author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Finding families

- Integrity: missing endpoints, unregistered kinds, invalid payloads, duplicate canonical identity, self-links, inverse mismatch.
- Ordering: genuine cycles, stale blockers, contradictory blocks pairs, unreachable prerequisites, redundant transitive edges.
- Coverage: unexplained roots or isolates, stories without delivery links, bodies naming absent prerequisites, milestones without outcomes, work without evidence lineage.
- Temporal: stale inferred relationships, superseded endpoints, snapshot drift, history and materialized-view disagreement.
- Quality: generic related overuse, alias drift, dense cliques, hub inflation, low-confidence edges without evidence.

## Policy stance

Sparse is not automatically bad and dense is not automatically good. Every finding must state the violated policy, evidence, confidence, consequences, and safe next action. Waivers are explicit, attributable, scoped, reviewable, and time-aware.
Relationship governance and completeness audit: semantic edge validation, graph coverage profiles, isolates, stale links, and remediation plans
Add policy-aware graph quality checks and machine-executable remediation planning without assuming that every root, isolate, or sparse item is defective.

Audits validate registered kinds, endpoints, direction and inverse rules, payload schema, cardinality, canonical aliases, duplicate identity, provenance, and snapshot consistency; Cycle checks operate only over declared order-bearing kinds and remain separate from associative strongly connected component analysis; Coverage profiles vary by item type, lifecycle state, project profile, age, and role; roots, archives, decisions, reminders, and scratch work can be explicitly valid isolates; Reports include counts, rates, severity, deterministic bounded samples, cursors, change since baseline, and machine-readable issue and remediation codes; Dry-run plans propose exact add, remove, retype, supersede, waive, or investigate operations with confidence and source evidence; no auto-fix invents an edge; Active and terminal remediation are separated; closed-item changes require dedicated changelog-safe batches and immutable audit history; Health, validate, stats, aggregate, and package audit surfaces reuse one SDK policy and finding contract with token-efficient default output
Make relationship quality observable, enforceable, explainable, and safely remediable across active and terminal history
Maintainers can distinguish genuine graph debt from intentional sparsity and repair the former without fabricating context
Prevents dangling, contradictory, cyclic, stale, or meaningless relationship graphs from misleading agents and algorithms
Counts-first audits, bounded samples, profile-aware policies, waivers, dry-run plans, and resumable remediation workflows
Current validation covers basic dependency integrity but the all-status audit exposed isolates, edge-kind drift, shallow ordering chains, and body-to-edge mismatches

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: existing validate,deps visualization,and one-time living-map audits do not provide schema-aware graph coverage profiles and remediation plans; this extends canonical governance rather than replacing those items.

message: Create relationship governance and completeness audit feature

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Manual relationship-density baseline (2026-07-14 pass #82) — the measurement this audit feature should automate. Method: dump .item.dependencies for every ACTIVE item + parent links; degree counts both endpoints; closed-item out-edges not counted (active-graph view). Before enrichment: 211 active items, 269 dep edges + 210 parent edges, 95 items with zero out-edges, 67 items with total degree <= 1 (parent-only). After a 67-edge related-kind enrichment batch: degree<=1 = 3 (pm-7zs0 reminder, pm-x2vx, pm-p5u6 — deliberate; no honest semantic partner). Enrichment structure worth encoding as audit heuristics: (1) scanner-finding clusters get a 2-level hub topology — members -> file-cluster representative -> rule-class representative (Semgrep non-literal-RegExp 30 items across 6 files; prototype-pollution 3; spawn-shell-true 4; dependabot-cooldown 2 under pm-oz8u); (2) story -> implementing-task edges (pm-6ixn -> pm-oz8u); (3) cross-epic complementarity edges documented in bodies but absent as edges (pm-n7rr <-> pm-usfg/pm-pbyu/pm-mpbb); (4) class-sibling edges from defect-family ledgers. Audit check candidates: degree<=1 ratio on active items, container out-degree zero, documented-in-body-but-unlinked pairs.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Verification evidence: linked governance suite passed; full repository coverage passed 5,798 tests at exact 100/100/100/100; lint/static/security/package gates passed. Fresh packed SDK audit and CLI temp project proved dependency/lifecycle blocked parity, graph assembly, semantic traversal, bounded path evidence, and zero history drift. This tranche is complete; broader type/profile baselines, change-since-baseline, cursors, executable dry-run plans, and all audit-surface integration remain canonical open scope.

A custom terminal holder regression proves missing_reference_terminal versus missing_reference_active classification. Full 5,800-test coverage remains exact 100/100/100/100.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Continue: pm graph audit subcommand surfaces the governance audit engine on the CLI/MCP boundary

patch.value: pm graph audit subcommand: CLI/MCP adapter over auditWorkspaceRelationshipGraph with sample and exempt-isolate controls

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress: the governance audit engine is now operable from the workspace surface via pm graph audit (CLI + pm_graph MCP + PmClient.graph). Counts-first envelope reports finding_count, findings_by_severity, findings_by_code (subject-count weighted), and the structural coverage profile; --sample bounds per-finding evidence, --exempt-isolate (repeatable/comma-separated) suppresses policy findings for deliberate isolates, --summary drops rows. Regression spec covers missing_reference_active + isolated_active_node detection, severity rollup consistency, and exemption behavior on a live temp workspace.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Remediation-surface addition via the graph-query-surface lane (2026-07-17): pm graph redundancy is the transitive-reduction detector this audit's stale/redundant-link remediation plans can consume — each finding carries the stored edge in its original spelling plus a semantic-orientation witness path, so remediation can propose removing shortcut edges with evidence. Live workspace scan found real blocked_by/blocks shortcut edges (bounded scan, truncated at --limit). Governance audit itself unchanged; redundancy stays a separate opt-in projection because shortcut edges are sometimes deliberate emphasis.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scope finding (2026-07-17 review, dogfooding pm graph audit): the audit reports 8 error-severity ordering_cycle findings spanning 49 items — ALL 49 verified terminal (closed/canceled legacy; e.g. pm-06t/pm-2xl Milestone-0 era, pm-0c0g/pm-8kxm, pm-0pvk/pm-s9hl pairs). validate lifecycle stays ok because it gates active holders. The graph audit needs the same legacy/active severity split validate's checks gained (pm-2ler dangling-refs precedent, pm-demq unknown-authors precedent): terminal-only cycles should downgrade to info with a legacy_* counter, error reserved for cycles touching >=1 active item. Never repair the closed items themselves (changelog drag). legacy_no_blocker_sentinel:98 + missing_reference_terminal:3 are the already-registered legacy sets (2ler ghosts) — audit correctly labels those legacy_/terminal already; ordering_cycle is the one code missing the split.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Second audit-scope gap (2026-07-17 review, code-read of sdk/graph): exact-duplicate stored edges are invisible to BOTH shipped surfaces. findRedundantRelationshipEdges orients blocked_by/blocks into one semantic family but its witness BFS skips the direct edge at depth 0 (analytics.ts expandWitnessFrontier), so a reciprocal stored pair (A blocked_by B AND B blocks A) or any parallel same-orientation edge yields no witness and is never reported — correct for transitive REDUCTION semantics, but nothing else catches the duplicate either: governance.ts audit codes are exactly {missing_reference_terminal, legacy_no_blocker_sentinel, ordering_cycle, stale_lifecycle_block, isolated_active_node, sparse_active_node} — no duplicate_edge code. Extension direction: add an info-severity duplicate_edge audit finding (same-family same-orientation parallel edges, reciprocal inverse spellings included), active-holders gated per the legacy-split policy in the previous note. Analytics semantics stay unchanged.

author: maintainer-agent
message: link legacy/active-split precedent: graph-audit ordering_cycle extension follows 2ler's active-gating pattern

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Census-method transition (2026-07-18 pass #98): the manual grep/dump density census from note 1 is now automated natively — `pm graph audit --summary` profile is the canonical baseline source going forward. Baseline 2026-07-18: nodes 1859, edges 3073 (parent 1681 / related 918 / blocked_by 353 / blocks 69 / discovered_from 36 / child 15 / incident_from 1), active_nodes 225, isolated_active_nodes 0, degree_leq_one_active_nodes 2 (deliberate pm-p5u6/pm-x2vx pair), missing_nodes 3 (known terminal ghosts). Historic grep-census figures (3,051 #92 / 3,091 #95 / 3,096 #97) used the manual method — the ~23-edge delta vs the native profile is method difference (declared-row grep vs registry graph), not edge loss; never reconcile the two series row-for-row.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Implementing ordering_cycle legacy/active split, duplicate_edge code, and dry-run remediation plans (pm graph plan)

value.note: planner op templates + truncation + cancellation spec

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress (graph-platform lane 2026-07-18): both recorded audit-scope gaps closed — (1) ordering_cycle now gates severity on lifecycle: cycles confined to terminal members downgrade to info legacy_ordering_cycle with changelog-safe remediation text, error reserved for cycles touching >=1 active item (2ler/demq legacy-split precedent applied as recorded in note 3); (2) new duplicate_edge / legacy_duplicate_edge info findings catch parallel same-family spellings including reciprocal blocked_by/blocks pairs, oriented via the shared analytics helpers (isTransitiveKind/orientTransitiveEdge/transitiveFamilyKey now exported — single implementation, no clones), endpoint-lifecycle gated per note 4. Additionally the first remediation-plan surface shipped: pm graph plan (12th subcommand, CLI+MCP+SDK) derives exact dry-run remove/retype/supersede/waive/investigate proposals with policy code, evidence, rationale, and confidence from audit findings plus redundancy witness rows — never auto-applied, never invents an edge. Verified live in a temp workspace: active cycle=error, terminal pair=legacy info, reciprocal pair=duplicate_edge.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Remaining canonical scope after the 2026-07-18 tranche (audit codes + pm graph plan): (1) coverage profiles varying by item type/lifecycle/age/role (today one policy with exempt-isolate escape hatch); (2) explicit attributable waiver records (plan proposes waive ops but nothing persists them); (3) resumable remediation workflows + changelog-safe closed-item batch tooling (plan is dry-run only by design); (4) reuse of the finding contract across health/validate/stats/aggregate/package-audit surfaces (today pm graph audit/plan only); (5) temporal findings (stale inferred relationships, snapshot drift). Baseline census impact: the 8 legacy-era ordering_cycle errors recorded in note 3 now report as info legacy_ordering_cycle — expect findings_by_severity.error to drop accordingly on the next native census; the duplicate_edge counters are new columns, not comparable to any prior series.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR#581 Greptile P1 disposition (2026-07-18): raw identical stored dependency rows (same holder, same kind, same target twice) are canonicalized away by RelationshipGraph edge-identity dedup BEFORE the graph audit runs, so duplicate_edge deliberately covers only cross-spelling parallels that survive canonicalization. Detecting raw same-row duplicates = the "duplicate canonical identity" integrity-family AC of this item and must scan pre-assembly item rows (validate/storage layer), not the assembled graph. Do not re-file — extend the integrity family here when that check is built.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #581 Greptile P1 follow-up: accepted review comment 3607265146. runGraphPlan now passes the public --limit bound into redundancyLimit, so callers raising the output limit above the planner's 25-row default receive all discoverable redundant-edge remediation proposals instead of a prematurely truncated plan. Regression builds a 28-node graph with 26 witnessed shortcuts and proves limit=100 returns every step without truncation; focused graph suite passes 21/21.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR#581 MERGED 2026-07-18: shipped from this item — lifecycle severity split (legacy_ordering_cycle informational vs active ordering_cycle error), duplicate_edge/legacy_duplicate_edge cross-spelling detection (shared orientation logic with analytics), and pm graph plan (12th subcommand) dry-run remediation with remove/retype/supersede/waive/investigate steps carrying policy code + evidence + confidence. Honest AC remainder: (a) raw same-row duplicate-identity integrity family at the pre-assembly storage layer (note 7 disposition — canonicalization hides these from the assembled graph); (b) coverage profiles by item type/lifecycle/age/role with explicit valid-isolate policy; (c) change-since-baseline deltas in reports; (d) health/validate/stats/aggregate/package-audit reusing the ONE SDK policy+finding contract; (e) changelog-safe closed-item remediation batches.
